title: Lowest Common Ancestor of a Binary Tree
coreIdea: Find the deepest node that is an ancestor of both given nodes. Recursively search left and right subtrees; if both subtrees return a match, the current node is the LCA, otherwise propagate whichever side found a match upward.
recognitionCues: asks for lowest common ancestor of two nodes in a tree
tree is not necessarily a BST (no ordering property to exploit)
approach: Recursively search left and right subtree for the two target nodes. If current node matches either target, return current node. If both left and right recursive calls return non-null, current node is the LCA. Otherwise return whichever side is non-null.
